_meta.source: Final list of Landscape Companies - Amadeus Consulting Project.xlsx

companies.name: Amenitiz
companies.whatTheyDo: Spanish independent-hotel platform — website builder, booking engine, channel manager, payments for small/boutique hotels. Backed by Eight Roads, Y Combinator. ~$50M+ raised total (undisclosed signal). Real category leader for indie hotels.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Channel-manager + direct-booking stack for indie hotels = same disintermediation pattern as Mews/Cloudbeds/SiteMinder. Each Amenitiz-managed hotel routes more bookings direct (and via Booking/Expedia connections) rather than through GDS-fronted distribution. Modest cumulative impact.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: STARTUP STAGE: Amenitiz helps independent hotels tackle a digital world PhocusWire

companies.name: Apaleo
companies.whatTheyDo: Open API-first hotel PMS platform built for the cloud era, popular with tech-forward independents and small chains in Europe. Marketplace model lets hotels plug in best-of-breed apps (booking engines, channel managers, payments) on top of Apaleo core. Interesting because the API-first PMS thesis directly challenges Amadeus's HotelPlatform/Opera-style monolithic approach.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Competes with Amadeus HotelPlatform on architecture and developer ecosystem. As hotels migrate from legacy PMSes to API-first cores like Apaleo, they assemble distribution and ops stacks that route around Amadeus's hotel content layer, weakening GDS pull on hotel inventory.
companies.amadeusThreat: Hospitality Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Open API cloud-native property management system for hotels

companies.name: Cloudbeds
companies.whatTheyDo: Cloud PMS and hospitality management platform for independent hotels, hostels, and small chains. Strong in SMB segments Oracle/Opera doesn't reach; integrated channel manager, booking engine, and payments. Interesting because PMS is the hotel-side data layer Amadeus's HotelPlatform competes against.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Competes directly with Amadeus HotelPlatform and iHotelier in the independent-hotel segment. As Cloudbeds layers distribution and direct-booking on its PMS, hotels can route inventory without sitting on Amadeus's hotel distribution stack, eroding GDS hotel content share.
companies.amadeusThreat: Hospitality Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Cloud hospitality platform unifying PMS, channel manager and booking engine

companies.name: DirectBooker
companies.whatTheyDo: Hotel direct-booking conversion tools — helps hotels drive direct bookings via website widgets, parity, and personalization. Phocuswire Hot 25 for 2026. Same competitive space as Triptease and Hotelchamp.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct-booking acceleration shifts share from OTAs and GDS-mediated channels to hotel direct — reducing the volume Amadeus's hospitality distribution business sees. If hotels increasingly succeed at direct, GDS-routed hotel bookings lose ground.
companies.amadeusThreat: Direct-booking conversion shifts hotel volume away from GDS-mediated distribution
companies.description: Phocuswire Hot 25 Travel Startups for 2026

companies.name: Guesty
companies.whatTheyDo: Guesty is the leading STR property-management platform — used by 200K+ STR units globally. Multiple acquisitions (MyVR, Rentals United etc.). NOT really a startup at $400M+ funding. Should be reclassified as Scale-up or Incumbent.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Guesty is the dominant operational layer for STR distribution — STRs distribute through Airbnb/Vrbo/Booking, never through GDS. Each Guesty-managed unit reinforces the non-GDS short-term-rental rail growing alongside hotels. Strategic competitive threat to hotel-GDS volumes.
companies.amadeusThreat: Emerging travel tech signal — may indicate shifting competitive dynamics for GDS providers
companies.description: Short-Term Rental Sector Sees More Consolidation as Tech Startup Guesty Buys Rival MyVR Skift

companies.name: Hospy
companies.whatTheyDo: LATAM-focused hospitality AI platform — 'digital employee' for independent hotels in Colombia and Brazil. Two years of product validation in those markets; planning 10X growth and LATAM expansion through 2026. Founder has 12+ years in hospitality tech. Product-led growth engine for local SMB hotels.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Plays in independent-hotel operations in LATAM where Amadeus's hotel platform footprint is thinner. As cloud-native AI-powered hotel ops layers proliferate (Hospy, Cloudbeds, Mews), the case for monolithic legacy hotel-platform stacks weakens.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI-native hotel ops layer in LATAM erodes case for legacy hotel platforms
companies.description: Phocuswire Hot 25 Travel Startups for 2026: Hospy

companies.name: Hotelmize
companies.whatTheyDo: Israeli hotel-revenue-management SaaS — uses AI to optimize hotel revenue. $12M Series B. Competes with IDeaS (SAS), Duetto, Atomize in hotel RMS.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Adjacent to Amadeus Hospitality (Demand360, RevenueStrategy360). Hotel RMS is a category Amadeus invests in — Hotelmize is one of several startups competing for the same hotel-revenue-tech budgets.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Traveltech startup Hotelmize raises $12 million Series B CTech

companies.name: Hotelogix
companies.whatTheyDo: Cloud hotel management for small and mid-size properties, popular in India, MEA, and SE Asia. PMS + channel manager + booking engine bundle aimed at independents and small chains. Interesting as a regional alternative to Cloudbeds/Mews in markets where Amadeus's hotel-IT presence is thinner.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Competes with Amadeus HotelPlatform in emerging-market independent hotel segments. Reduces the addressable pool of hotels Amadeus could sign as IT customers, and pushes inventory through Hotelogix's own distribution rather than GDS-led channels.
companies.amadeusThreat: Hospitality Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Cloud-based property management system for small hotels

companies.name: HyperGuest
companies.whatTheyDo: Israeli hotel-content connectivity / direct-distribution platform — connects hotels to distributors via API, focus on disintermediating wholesalers and GDS for hotel inventory. $23M Series A. Interesting as the active-going version of Impala's thesis.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct GDS alternative for hotel content aggregation — distributors using HyperGuest reduce dependence on Amadeus / Hotelbeds-style hotel content. If HyperGuest scales hotel-direct connectivity, GDS hotel volumes decline.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: HyperGuest raises $23 million Series A to connect hotels, suppliers, and travel distributors CTech

companies.name: Katanox
companies.whatTheyDo: Hotel-distribution and payments API platform — modernizes hotel connectivity for distributors. Pitched as direct alternative to legacy GDS hotel content. Same category as HyperGuest, Impala.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct GDS-replacement thesis for hotel content. Each Katanox-connected distributor reduces dependence on Amadeus Hospitality and traditional channel managers. Modest scale today; reinforces structural disintermediation pattern.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: STARTUP STAGE: Katanox aims to simplify hotel distribution PhocusWire

companies.name: Lighthouse
companies.whatTheyDo: Commercial intelligence and revenue optimization platform for hotels and short-term rentals (merger of OTA Insight + Transparent). Pricing, market intelligence, parity, business intelligence — used by 65K+ hotels. Recent €351M round (Permira-led) signals scale-up status. Interesting because it sits at the data layer that informs hotel pricing decisions GDS systems then distribute.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Adjacent rather than direct: Lighthouse informs the pricing that flows through GDS hotel channels but doesn't replace distribution. Could become more strategic if it extends into distribution orchestration. Amadeus competes via Amadeus Demand360 / RevenueStrategy360 in this space.
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC adoption accelerates — airlines moving to direct retail, core threat to GDS model
companies.description: London-based Lighthouse secures €351 million to accelerate growth of its commercial intelligence platform eu-startups.com

companies.name: Mews
companies.whatTheyDo: Cloud-native hotel property management system (PMS) — the operational core of independent hotels and small chains. 5,500+ properties, 85+ countries. Acquired DataChat (AI), bought competitors to consolidate the modern PMS layer. Interesting because the PMS is the system of record for hotel inventory — whoever owns it controls how that inventory flows to distribution channels.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Strategic adjacency: Mews PMS-controlled inventory increasingly flows through channel managers Mews owns or partners with — direct connections to Booking.com, Expedia, sometimes bypassing GDS-mediated hotel content (HRS, etc.). Long-term risk to Amadeus Hospitality Solutions / Amadeus hotel content.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Hotel tech company Mews acquires AI startup DataChat to boost automation Hotel Management Network

companies.name: NUMA
companies.whatTheyDo: European “branded apartment hotel” operator — acquires legacy hotel real estate, rebrands and operates as digital-first short-stay. Berlin HQ. $45M growth round. Interesting as the German answer to Sonder — owns supply and distributes direct + selective OTA.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Owned-supply operators (Numa, Sonder, citizenM) bypass GDS for inventory distribution — typically run their own websites + Booking.com + Airbnb. As branded-apartment-hotel category scales, that's hotel-night inventory not flowing through Amadeus Hospitality.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Boutique hotel tech platform NUMA raises $45M growth financing round led by DN Capital TechCrunch

companies.name: RateGain
companies.whatTheyDo: RateGain is a publicly-listed Indian hospitality-distribution and revenue-management company — NOT a startup. Major incumbent. SaaS distribution + RMS + channel manager for hotels. Direct competitor to several Amadeus Hospitality products.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Material competitor to Amadeus Hospitality — RateGain owns channel manager, RMS, and distribution analytics share that Amadeus also targets. Should be reclassified as Incumbent, not startup.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: RateGain, Arpón Enterprise Launch Integrated Hotel Tech Platform, Shares End Flat Equitypandit

companies.name: Selfbook
companies.whatTheyDo: US-based hotel-payment platform — one-click direct hotel bookings with embedded payments. AmEx-backed. Lets hotels capture direct bookings with frictionless checkout, bypassing OTAs. Real player in hotel-direct payments space.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct adjacency to Amadeus Outpayce and hotel-direct booking. Each Selfbook-powered direct booking is one less OTA-mediated (and thus one less Amadeus-relevant) transaction. Modest individual impact; meaningful as part of broader hotel-direct shift.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: American Express invests in hotel payment startup Selfbook PhocusWire

companies.name: SiteMinder
companies.whatTheyDo: Channel manager, booking engine, and distribution platform for hotels — connects properties to OTAs, GDSs, metasearch, and direct channels. Public company (ASX-listed), 40,000+ hotel customers. Interesting because SiteMinder sits between hotels and Amadeus's hotel content layer, controlling which inventory flows where.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Major gatekeeper in hotel distribution. Hotels using SiteMinder can route inventory selectively, and as SiteMinder builds direct-booking and AI tools, it strengthens hotels' ability to optimize away from Amadeus-aligned GDS channels. Strategic relationship and competitive pressure simultaneously.
companies.amadeusThreat: Hospitality Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Hotel channel manager and distribution platform

companies.name: Smartness
companies.whatTheyDo: Italian hospitality tech for independent hotels and B&Bs — PMS, channel management, direct-booking website tools. €47M Series B (2026) for European expansion. Interesting as a SMB-hotel-stack consolidator (PMS + channel mgr + booking engine) in the underserved European independent-hotel segment.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Channel manager + PMS combo means Smartness controls how its hotels' inventory flows to OTAs and meta sources. Could route inventory away from GDS-mediated channels (HRS, Amadeus Hospitality content) toward direct OTA APIs. Long-term distribution share risk in independent-hotel segment.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Italian TravelTech startup Smartness closes €47 million Series B for European expansion EU-Startups

companies.name: Stayflexi
companies.whatTheyDo: Operating system for hotels and vacation rentals — PMS, channel manager, contactless ops, payments. India-strong with global ambitions. Interesting as another API-first cloud PMS competing in the Apaleo/Cloudbeds segment.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Same dynamic as Cloudbeds and Apaleo — competes with Amadeus HotelPlatform in independent and SMB hotel/STR segments. Erodes hotel-side IT mindshare and creates parallel distribution stacks that may or may not include GDS connectivity.
companies.amadeusThreat: Hospitality Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Flexible booking PMS for hotels and vacation rentals

companies.name: Wayfarer
companies.whatTheyDo: Japanese hotel-tech startup positioning as an 'AWS of hotels' — cloud infrastructure and APIs for hospitality. Interesting because it represents another API-first hotel-platform thesis in the Apaleo/Mews mold, this time in APAC.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Same dynamic as Apaleo and Mews — API-first hotel platforms erode the case for monolithic Amadeus HotelPlatform. Limited scale today but reinforces the architectural shift hotels are making away from legacy systems Amadeus belongs to.
companies.amadeusThreat: Travel Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Japanese travel platform

companies.name: Yanolja
companies.whatTheyDo: Yanolja is South Korea's largest travel super-app (hotels, leisure, mobility) and a $1B+ company — SoftBank-backed unicorn that owns hotel-tech assets including a major PMS business. NOT a startup. Should be reclassified as Incumbent.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Yanolja is a strategic player in hotel PMS and Asian travel distribution, including via its acquisitions of Innsoft and Go Global. Direct competitor to Amadeus Hospitality in PMS and Asian hotel distribution; substantial threat over the long term. Reclassify as Incumbent.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Softbank to Buy Stake in South Korean Travel Startup Yanolja Skift

companies.name: Accelya
companies.whatTheyDo: Airline retailing platform (FLX ONE) processing 31 billion NDC offers daily and settling $100B+ annually for 200+ airlines; acquired Farelogix 2020
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Single largest direct competitor in airline retailing — American Airlines contract is a direct exclusion of Amadeus from the world's largest carrier
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — Critical threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Airline retailing platform (FLX ONE) processing 31 billion NDC offers daily and settling $100B+ annually for 200+ airlines; acquired Farelogix 2020

companies.name: AirGateway
companies.whatTheyDo: NDC aggregator targeting the corporate/TMC channel with aggregated content from 35+ airlines
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Attacks the corporate booking channel — historically Amadeus's highest-margin segment — with NDC-native content
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — Medium threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: NDC aggregator targeting the corporate/TMC channel with aggregated content from 35+ airlines

companies.name: Cirium
companies.whatTheyDo: Aviation analytics and data platform (LexisNexis-owned, ex-FlightGlobal). Sells flight schedules, on-time performance, fleet, and traffic data to airlines, airports, OTAs, and financial markets. Interesting because aviation data is an information layer adjacent to Amadeus's own travel-intelligence businesses (Demand360, Cirium-style products via ForwardKeys etc.).
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Indirect competitor in travel data and analytics. As airlines and OTAs buy schedule and operational data from Cirium, they reduce dependency on Amadeus-owned data products. Limited direct GDS impact but erodes Amadeus's broader 'travel intelligence' positioning.
companies.amadeusThreat: Aviation Data player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Aviation analytics and travel data provider

companies.name: Datalex
companies.whatTheyDo: Airline Offer & Order Management System (Stellex) live at easyJet, Virgin Australia, and LATAM; raised EUR 25M in 2024
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Directly competes with Amadeus Offers & Orders — airlines use Datalex to escape PSS/GDS vendor lock-in
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — High threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Airline Offer & Order Management System (Stellex) live at easyJet, Virgin Australia, and LATAM; raised EUR 25M in 2024

companies.name: Distribusion
companies.whatTheyDo: Berlin-based ground-transport API and marketplace — bus, train, ferry connections across Europe / globally. €30M raise. Powers Trainline, Omio, Google Travel for ground content. Interesting as the de facto ground-transport aggregator behind much of European multimodal search.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct architectural alternative to Amadeus Rail for ground/rail content aggregation. Distribusion's API is what many ground-content distributors integrate — if it remains the dominant rail/bus aggregator, Amadeus's rail content ambitions face an entrenched incumbent.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI-powered booking could disintermediate traditional GDS — travelers skip Amadeus-powered channels
companies.description: Berlin-based travel marketplace Distribusion raises €30 million to make it easier to book transport online EU-Startups

companies.name: Duffel
companies.whatTheyDo: Modern NDC + airline content aggregation API — “Stripe for flights.” Used by travel-builders to embed flight booking with one API call. Pre-funded acquisition target speculation. Direct GDS architectural alternative for the developer-builder community.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: One of the most direct architectural threats to GDS in the long tail of distribution: every new travel startup, super-app, or fintech embedding flights uses Duffel rather than building GDS connections. Each Duffel transaction is a flight booking that doesn't pay Amadeus a content/distribution fee.
companies.amadeusThreat: Direct GDS competitor — modern API layer for airline content that replaces legacy GDS connections
companies.description: Duffel raises $41M Series B to modernize airline distribution APIs

companies.name: Journera
companies.whatTheyDo: Cross-industry traveler-data platform — connects airlines, hotels, and TMC data into a single timeline for in-trip personalization and disruption management. $11M. Backed by United, American, Hyatt. Quietly strategic: could become the data layer airlines use without GDS.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Potentially a long-term architectural threat: if Journera becomes the cross-supplier traveler-data backbone, GDS's role as the connective tissue between suppliers weakens. Airlines could share data via Journera and run distribution outside Amadeus's data infrastructure. Watch closely.
companies.amadeusThreat: Cross-industry traveler data platform could replace GDS as the data backbone
companies.description: Journera raises $11M to connect traveler data across airlines, hotels, and agencies

companies.name: Meili
companies.whatTheyDo: Mobility distribution / ground transport ancillary platform for airlines and travel brands. Connects ~40 partners including Lufthansa, LATAM, Accor, Enterprise and Budget. Founded 2022 by ex-CarTrawler executives Mike McGearty (CEO) and Bobby Healy. Raised €20M+ total, including €8.1M led by Schooner Capital and Tribal Ventures (Dec 2024). Building direct-model alternative to traditional car-rental aggregation.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct mobility distribution disintermediates legacy aggregators in a category Amadeus serves through its mobility/ancillary distribution offerings. If Meili's direct model becomes the standard, Amadeus risks losing the connecting layer for ground content sold through airlines and OTAs.
companies.amadeusThreat: Direct mobility distribution model replaces aggregator middleware
companies.description: Phocuswire Hot 25 Travel Startups for 2026: Meili

companies.name: myPlane
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-native flight search and booking platform pitched as a GDS alternative for the next-generation traveler. Named in Amadeus Top 9 Startups 2026. Sits in the same architectural-alternative bucket as Duffel and Wenrix — modern API stack between airline content and the AI front door.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct architectural alternative to GDS-mediated flight distribution. If myPlane and peers build credible NDC-first aggregation and AI-native booking experiences, OTAs and AI agents can bypass Amadeus Travel Distribution for flight content.
companies.amadeusThreat: Architectural alternative to GDS for AI-era flight distribution
companies.description: Top 9 Travel Startups in 2026 - Amadeus

companies.name: Mystifly
companies.whatTheyDo: Indian B2B air-content aggregator — wholesale flight content to OTAs and travel agents, owns ARC/IATA endorsements. $8M Series B. Operates as a low-cost alternative to GDS for flight content in emerging markets.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct architectural alternative to GDS air-content for OTAs/agents in emerging markets. Each agency using Mystifly for flight content potentially routes around Amadeus. Small share globally but emblematic of NDC-era content aggregation outside the GDS layer.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Travel tech startup Mystifly raises $8 million in funding from Cornerstone Venture Partners, others The Economic Times

companies.name: Paxport
companies.whatTheyDo: Cloud-based NDC distribution platform; became Ryanair's first approved OTA aggregator in 2024
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Sets replicable template for airline-controlled GDS bypass — if other airlines follow, Amadeus loses distribution rights
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — Medium threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: Cloud-based NDC distribution platform; became Ryanair's first approved OTA aggregator in 2024

companies.name: RateHawk
companies.whatTheyDo: B2B hotel rate aggregation with $3.7B GTV in 2024 (67% YoY growth) serving travel agents and OTAs across 100+ countries
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Competes directly with Amadeus hotel distribution at $3.7B scale and growing 67% YoY — no disclosed VC pressure, entirely organic growth
companies.amadeusThreat: Distribution & APIs player — High threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: B2B hotel rate aggregation with $3.7B GTV in 2024 (67% YoY growth) serving travel agents and OTAs across 100+ countries

companies.name: Sabre (Mosaic)
companies.whatTheyDo: Sabre Mosaic AI-native platform launched ITB Berlin March 2026 ('once-in-a-generation rebuild'). Sabre MCP server in production. Backbone of Mindtrip+PayPal agentic flight booking (May 2026). 420 airlines, 2M+ hotels exposed via agentic APIs. Sabre repositioned the whole company as AI-native and shipped the FIRST proven end-to-end agentic GDS+payments+AI-front-door stack.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct head-to-head competitor on every distribution and PSS line. We carve Sabre as an ENABLING LAYER for cross-industry disruptors — not the disruptor itself, but the rail that makes Mindtrip+PayPal work. Competitor posture.
companies.amadeusThreat: Severe. Core GDS distribution (air content). PSS / Altea. Hotel content. Distribution fee economics.
companies.description: Sabre Mosaic AI-native platform ITB Berlin March 2026; MCP server in prod

companies.name: Talixo
companies.whatTheyDo: B2B ground transfer marketplace aggregating chauffeur and taxi suppliers globally, sold to OTAs, TMCs, and corporates as an API/white-label. Interesting because ground transfers are an ancillary category Amadeus has tried to address through partnerships but doesn't own.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus's ground-transportation content and partnerships (CarTrawler-style integrations). If Talixo wins B2B distribution deals with TMCs and OTAs, Amadeus loses the ancillary attach on transfers within the trip basket.
companies.amadeusThreat: Ground Transport player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: B2B ground transportation aggregator for travel sellers

companies.name: TBO Tek
companies.whatTheyDo: B2B travel distribution connecting 160,000+ travel agents and corporates globally to hotel, air, and ancillary inventory via API
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Largest B2B travel API platform in India by agent count — parallel distribution network competing with Amadeus for agency content delivery in South Asia
companies.amadeusThreat: Distribution & APIs player — High threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: B2B travel distribution connecting 160,000+ travel agents and corporates globally to hotel, air, and ancillary inventory via API

companies.name: TPConnects
companies.whatTheyDo: IATA-certified NDC aggregation platform (Astra for airlines, Iris for sellers) backed by Flight Centre
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Backed by one of the world's largest travel retailers; strong Middle East and South Asia footprint where Amadeus is investing
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — High threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: IATA-certified NDC aggregation platform (Astra for airlines, Iris for sellers) backed by Flight Centre

companies.name: Travelport
companies.whatTheyDo: Third major legacy GDS alongside Amadeus and Sabre. Owns Galileo, Apollo, Worldspan platforms. Acquired by Siris/Elliott (private equity) in 2019; relisted/restructured under new leadership. Travelport+ multi-source platform (NDC + EDIFACT + LCC content). Launched Travelport Engage AI/agentic tools 2024-2025. Long-standing relationship with TMCs (CWT, FCM, ATPI) and OTAs. Limited public moves on agent-protocol stack (UCP, AP2, ACP) relative to Sabre Mosaic.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct head-to-head competitor on every distribution and PSS line — the original three-way GDS race. If Travelport ships an agent-native rebuild like Sabre Mosaic, the GDS market consolidates around two AI-native players (Sabre + Travelport) with Amadeus as third. If Travelport falls behind on agentic infra, Sabre becomes the de facto Amadeus alternative for AI agents and Travelport becomes the slower, traditional choice. Competitor posture.
companies.amadeusThreat: Severe. Core GDS distribution (air content), NDC aggregation, hotel content, distribution fee economics. Direct competitor on every line Amadeus serves.
companies.description: Travelport — third legacy GDS (Galileo/Apollo/Worldspan)

companies.name: Verteil Technologies
companies.whatTheyDo: NDC aggregator (Verteil Direct Connect) connecting 50+ airlines including Air India, Turkish Airlines, and Riyadh Air to travel sellers globally
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Directly competes with Amadeus NDC connectivity — a leaner airline-controlled alternative to Amadeus distribution
companies.amadeusThreat: NDC & Airline Retailing player — High threat — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: NDC aggregator (Verteil Direct Connect) connecting 50+ airlines including Air India, Turkish Airlines, and Riyadh Air to travel sellers globally

companies.name: Vervotech
companies.whatTheyDo: Hotel content aggregator / mapping platform — deduplicates and matches hotel inventory across suppliers. Acquired by Juniper Group (a major B2B travel-tech consolidator). Direct competitor to Amadeus hotel content matching.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct adjacency to Amadeus's hotel-content layer. Hotel mapping/dedup is core infrastructure for B2B distributors; Juniper's acquisition of Vervotech entrenches a non-Amadeus alternative for hotel content normalization.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Juniper Group Acquires Hotel Tech Startup Vervotech Skift

companies.name: Wenrix
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-powered flight booking infrastructure (NDC, content aggregation, fare optimization). Acquired by Etraveli in 2026 — strategic move by a major OTA-backbone player to insource AI flight retail. Israeli team, small but technically deep. Interesting because the acquisition signals that mid-market OTAs are building/buying their own AI-native flight booking stacks rather than relying on GDS.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Wenrix-as-acquired-tech accelerates Etraveli's ability to disintermediate GDS in flight content aggregation. Pattern repeats — OTA backbones (Etraveli, Hopper Cloud, Duffel) are building parallel rails to GDS for flight retail. Amadeus loses flight transaction fees as these alternative stacks scale.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI-powered booking could disintermediate traditional GDS — travelers skip Amadeus-powered channels
companies.description: Etraveli acquires Israeli AI flight booking startup Wenrix in $200-$300 million deal CTech

companies.name: Blockskye
companies.whatTheyDo: Blockchain infrastructure for corporate travel. Direct-to-supplier booking, payments and expense management that bypasses traditional travel agencies and credit-card-network intermediaries. Clients include PwC, Tripadvisor and Diageo. KAYAK for Business partnership. Claims 14.5% travel cost reduction and 84% agent-spend reduction. Boston, founded 2017. $33M raised total ($15.8M Series C July 2025 led by Blockchange, with United Airlines Ventures, Lightspeed Faction, etc.). Launching stablecoin-based payments.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct architectural threat to GDS-mediated corporate travel. If blockchain-native rails for direct-supplier bookings and settlement scale, GDS economics in the corporate channel shrink. United Airlines Ventures backing signals airlines see this as a viable alternative to current distribution rails.
companies.amadeusThreat: Blockchain rails for direct-supplier corporate travel replace GDS-mediated booking and settlement
companies.description: Blockskye raises $15.8M Series C for blockchain travel infrastructure

companies.name: Crisis24
companies.whatTheyDo: Consolidated enterprise duty of care platform (Horizon) rolling up WorldAware, OnSolve, and Anvil Group

companies.name: Dfinitiv
companies.whatTheyDo: Loyalty-led commerce media platform. SmartCXP combines personalization, intelligent loyalty offers and content aggregation across mobile and digital channels for travel companies, financial services and credit card issuers. United Airlines Ventures took an equity stake Aug 2025 to explore MileagePlus + Kinective Media applications. Cross-industry applicability across loyalty audiences.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Sits adjacent to airline distribution at the merchandising/loyalty layer. If carriers monetize loyalty through Dfinitiv-style commerce media networks instead of through GDS-mediated retail, Amadeus loses share of the ancillary-retailing economics that Altea Merchandising aims to capture.
companies.amadeusThreat: Airline loyalty/commerce media routed outside GDS-mediated retailing
companies.description: Amadeus Top 9 Travel Startups 2026: Dfinitiv

companies.name: Flutterwave
companies.whatTheyDo: Pan-African payments infrastructure (cards, transfers, FX) used by travel platforms, OTAs, and airlines to collect and settle in 30+ African currencies. Strong rails for cross-border travel commerce in a region underserved by Visa/Mastercard reach. Interesting because settlement is a chokepoint Amadeus B2B Wallet competes in.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus B2B Wallet and Outpayce in emerging-market travel settlement. As African OTAs, TMCs, and airlines adopt Flutterwave for collections, Amadeus loses the payments leg of the value chain even when GDS still handles the booking.
companies.amadeusThreat: Payments player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: African payments infrastructure for cross-border travel transactions

companies.name: Hands In
companies.whatTheyDo: Group payment platform for travel — splits payments across multiple payers for group bookings (events, tours, hotels). API embedded into travel checkout flows. Named in Amadeus Top 9 Startups 2026.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Specialized payment rail for group travel sits adjacent to Outpayce's core remit. Embedded group-payment flows route around traditional travel settlement infrastructure for an important segment (group, events, MICE).
companies.amadeusThreat: Group-payment rails bypass traditional travel-payment infrastructure in MICE / group bookings
companies.description: Top 9 Travel Startups in 2026 - Amadeus

companies.name: Indicio
companies.whatTheyDo: Decentralized identity / verifiable credentials platform (Indicio Proven). Powers ICAO Digital Travel Credentials — used by SITA and the Government of Aruba for smartphone-based passport-free border entry. NEC X invested Nov 2025. Recently launched Proven AI for Digital Travel — lets AI agents and chatbots cryptographically verify traveler identity at the start of a booking conversation. Travel ID infrastructure layer.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Adjacent rather than directly competitive. Indicio operates a layer Amadeus does not own — traveler identity rails. As airlines, governments and AI travel agents adopt verifiable credentials, Amadeus's PSS/distribution stack will need to integrate with this layer. If Indicio becomes the de facto identity standard, it shifts where 'know your traveler' value accrues.
companies.amadeusThreat: Identity layer becomes a new integration point for PSS and distribution
companies.description: Phocuswire Hot 25 Travel Startups for 2026: Indicio

companies.name: JPMorgan Chase
companies.whatTheyDo: Chase Travel platform (Booking.com-powered) integrated with Ultimate Rewards. 65M+ Chase Sapphire cardholders eligible. Continued investment in proprietary trip-planning UX. Loyalty + booking + payments bundled. Routes through Booking.com (potential Amadeus exposure via Booking) but increasingly tries to own end-to-end relationship.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Less AI-forward than Capital One but larger cardholder base. Neutral posture.
companies.amadeusThreat: Moderate. OTA channel volume (Booking indirect).
companies.description: Chase Travel (Booking.com-powered); 65M+ Sapphire cardholders

companies.name: Klarna
companies.whatTheyDo: AP2 partnership with Google for agent-driven payments. Direct partnerships: Expedia (US/UK/DE/SE/FI), Minor Hotels (13 EU countries), Lufthansa Group (via Adyen). Built own Agentic Product Protocol (Dec 2025). BNPL becoming agentic-checkout-native. Routes traveler payments + intent data outside any GDS — exposes travel inventory to AI agents via its own protocol.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Klarna is the most aggressive non-travel fintech in agent commerce. Their own protocol is a direct competitor to Amadeus owning the agent-commerce layer for travel payments. Substitute posture.
companies.amadeusThreat: High. Outpayce, Amadeus Insurance, ancillary attach revenue. Own agentic protocol competes for agent-commerce layer.
companies.description: Klarna AP2 with Google; Expedia/Minor/Lufthansa partnerships; own Agentic Product Protocol Dec 2025

companies.name: PayPal
companies.whatTheyDo: Mindtrip + Sabre + PayPal agentic flight booking stack live since May 6, 2026. Buy Now Pay Later integrated into Mindtrip checkout. AP2 launch partner. The payment+identity layer in the canonical AI-front-door+GDS+payments stack. Supplies trusted commerce, agentic identity, and BNPL flexibility that lets AI agents complete travel bookings inside chat.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: PayPal is the FIRST proof point that a non-traditional travel actor is critical to the agentic booking stack — it bridges Sabre content to AI front doors. No public Amadeus equivalent. Substitute for Outpayce in agent commerce.
companies.amadeusThreat: High. Outpayce (Amadeus payments), embedded fintech revenue in agentic flows. Bridges GDS content to AI front doors via AP2.
companies.description: Mindtrip + Sabre + PayPal agentic flight booking, May 6 2026

companies.name: Revolut
companies.whatTheyDo: Revolut Stays (hotels) integrated in banking app. Travel insurance, FX. 45M+ users. AI features expanding. European neobank with rapidly growing travel commerce footprint. Pattern matches Capital One in Europe.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Strategically the most relevant European neobank for the Amadeus base. Worth tracking AI agent strategy. Substitute posture.
companies.amadeusThreat: Moderate. European OTA channel volume.
companies.description: Revolut Stays + travel insurance + FX in 45M-user banking app

companies.name: Stripe
companies.whatTheyDo: Co-creator with OpenAI of Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP). Stripe + Mindtrip-like models for any AI agent to charge cards/wallets autonomously. The dominant card-payment rail for AI agents. Most consumer AI startups default to Stripe. Sets the economics of agent commerce.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: If most AI travel commerce settles via Stripe + ACP, Amadeus Outpayce loses the agentic commerce share. Stripe + Booking.com is a quiet, deep relationship. Substitute / Enabler.
companies.amadeusThreat: High. Outpayce in card-acceptance flows. ACP becomes the default agent-payment rail.
companies.description: Stripe + OpenAI Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP)

companies.name: Swiipr
companies.whatTheyDo: Crew payments and disruption-compensation platform for airlines — instant card-based payouts to crew and passengers during irregular operations (IRROPS). Named in Amadeus Top 9 Startups 2026. Sits in the disruption-servicing slice that Amadeus's Altea Departure Control touches.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Airline operations and disruption servicing is an Amadeus stronghold via Altea. Swiipr's modern, card-based payout rails compete with traditional airline back-office settlement and could be integrated by airlines as an alternative to GDS-managed servicing flows.
companies.amadeusThreat: Modern crew/passenger payout rails compete with legacy airline back-office and Altea-mediated servicing
companies.description: Top 9 Travel Startups in 2026 - Amadeus

companies.name: BookingData
companies.whatTheyDo: Airline ancillary-revenue platform — helps airlines surface and sell ancillaries. Undisclosed funding. Same competitive space as Gordian Software.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Airline retailing / merchandising layer — directly overlaps with where Amadeus Nevio invests. If BookingData wins airline ancillary deals, Amadeus loses ancillary-attach influence.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI-powered booking could disintermediate traditional GDS — travelers skip Amadeus-powered channels
companies.description: STARTUP STAGE: BookingData helps airlines tap into ancillary revenue PhocusWire

companies.name: Fetcherr
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-native dynamic pricing and revenue management for airlines using large market models. Pitched as the next-gen replacement for PROS/Sabre AirVision-style RM systems. Strong traction with Azul, JetBlue, and others. Interesting because RM/pricing is an airline-IT layer adjacent to Amadeus's Altea/Nevio offering.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus Revenue Management and Sabre's RM portfolio in the airline-IT stack. If Fetcherr wins pricing/RM mandates, it weakens Amadeus's IT-side relationship with airlines and creates wedge into broader passenger services systems where Amadeus monetizes today.
companies.amadeusThreat: Airline Tech player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: AI-powered dynamic pricing and revenue management for airlines

companies.name: Flyr
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-native airline revenue management and offer/order platform. Pioneers “dynamic offer management” — personalized pricing and bundling at point of sale. Powers carriers like JetBlue, Avianca. Interesting because it competes head-on with Amadeus Altea Revenue Management and Amadeus's Nevio Offer/Order roadmap.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus in the airline IT stack — specifically revenue management and offer construction. If FLYR continues winning airline customers, Amadeus loses both license revenue (Altea) and influence over how airline content is priced and merchandised downstream. Affects both PSS and distribution layers.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI-native revenue management competes with Amadeus Altéa revenue tools
companies.description: FLYR raises $150M Series C for AI airline revenue management platform

companies.name: Gordian Software
companies.whatTheyDo: Airline-ancillary merchandising platform — helps airlines sell baggage, seats, bundles via APIs to any distribution channel (NDC, OTAs, direct). $30M raise. Strategic acquisition candidate. Interesting because it sits exactly where Amadeus's Nevio offer/order roadmap is investing.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus on airline retailing / offer-order merchandising. If Gordian wins airline ancillary infrastructure deals, Amadeus loses ancillary-attach influence and revenue, which is a core part of Nevio's value proposition.
companies.amadeusThreat: If ancillary merchandising moves to supplier-side APIs, Amadeus loses retailing revenue
companies.description: Gordian Software raises $30M to power airline ancillary merchandising via APIs

companies.name: Hopper
companies.whatTheyDo: Mobile-first OTA known for price-prediction and fintech-style fare freeze / cancel-for-any-reason products. Now monetizes its B2B fintech stack (Hopper Cloud) to power third-party OTAs and airlines. ~$5B valuation peak. Interesting because of the white-label fintech distribution play: airlines and OTAs route ancillaries through Hopper, not the GDS.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Twofold impact: (1) consumer app captures discovery + booking demand away from GDS-powered OTAs; (2) Hopper Cloud B2B competes directly with Amadeus on ancillary merchandising, fintech (price-lock, CFAR) and modern retailing — areas Amadeus has been investing in via Nevio.
companies.amadeusThreat: Google building native travel booking in AI Mode — captures demand before it reaches GDS-powered OTAs
companies.description: Hopper Technology Solutions launches AI-based cancelation program Hotel Management

companies.name: Hopper Cloud
companies.whatTheyDo: B2B fintech arm of Hopper — sells fintech products (cancel-for-any-reason, price freeze, flexible payments) embedded in third-party OTA and airline flows. Interesting because Hopper Cloud is monetizing the booking-flow itself with ancillary financial products that travel-tech platforms layer in.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Indirect competitive overlap with Amadeus's NDC servicing and ancillary distribution. Hopper Cloud's CFAR/freeze products sit alongside (or instead of) airline-native ancillaries that Amadeus distributes — fragmenting where the traveler buys flexibility from. Also competitive with Amadeus B2B Wallet on payments rails.
companies.amadeusThreat: Travel Tech B2B player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: B2B fintech and travel distribution platform from Hopper

companies.name: Humoniq
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-native BPO for travel and transport — deploys AI agents to handle servicing, support, and operations workflows for travel companies. Interesting because servicing/support is a high-cost slice of the value chain Amadeus's GDS-fed contact center stacks have served via humans.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Adjacent to Amadeus's airline IT and TMC servicing offerings — if Humoniq's AI agents handle a meaningful share of post-booking servicing (changes, refunds, irregular ops), it compresses the value of Amadeus's servicing tools and PNR-management workflows.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI / Automation player — see full profile in Startup Repository
companies.description: AI customer service automation platform

companies.name: Kaiban
companies.whatTheyDo: AI agent management system purpose-built for airlines. Enables airlines to launch and manage AI-powered agents across pricing, revenue management, airport operations, customer experience, maintenance, and ecommerce. Phocuswire Hot 25 for 2026. Designed to keep airline-owned knowledge bases under airline control rather than ceded to OTAs or GDSs.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Indirect but meaningful threat to GDS leverage. Kaiban explicitly positions itself as helping airlines retain valuable passenger data and institutional knowledge historically ceded to OTAs and GDSs. If airlines build their own AI agent operations on Kaiban, GDSs lose influence over how airline content is merchandised and distributed in the AI era.
companies.amadeusThreat: Airline-owned AI agent stack reduces airline dependence on GDS-mediated distribution
companies.description: Phocuswire Hot 25 Travel Startups for 2026: Kaiban

companies.name: Opfyx
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-powered aviation operations platform — helps aviation teams plan, manage and execute operations across airports, airlines and ground handlers. Covers rostering, staff allocation, scheduled vs disrupted operations, internal/external communications, airspace and stand/escalator management. Vantage Group's first Vantage Futures CVC investment; piloted at JFK Airport Terminal 7.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Plays in the airline/airport operations control layer adjacent to Amadeus Altea departure control and operations products. As airports move to AI-native ops platforms, Amadeus risks ceding the operations workflow layer that traditionally bundled with its airline IT footprint.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI ops layer for airports/airlines that overlaps with Altea operations products
companies.description: Amadeus Top 9 Travel Startups 2026: Opfyx

companies.name: Overwatch AI
companies.whatTheyDo: AI aviation operations platform for pilots and airline crews — flight planning, fatigue management, ops workflows. Interesting because it's part of the broader airline-IT ecosystem (alongside Amadeus Altea Flight Ops, Sabre AirOps) where Amadeus competes.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Small but direct overlap with Amadeus Altea Flight Operations and crew/ops modules. If Overwatch and similar AI-native ops tools gain airline customers, Amadeus's airline-IT cross-sell story weakens, particularly with smaller carriers shopping best-of-breed.
companies.amadeusThreat: AI / Operations player — see full profile in Startup Repository

companies.name: PROS
companies.whatTheyDo: Established airline revenue management and dynamic pricing software (publicly traded). Long-standing competitor to Sabre AirVision and Amadeus's own RM offerings. Interesting because PROS is the incumbent Fetcherr and other AI-native pricing startups are trying to displace, and a major component of the airline-IT competitive set.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor to Amadeus Revenue Management in the airline-IT stack. PROS wins on enterprise track record and product depth; as airlines evaluate AI-native RM (Fetcherr, etc.), the three-way pricing market (PROS, Sabre, Amadeus) is being disrupted by a fourth wave.
companies.amadeusThreat: Revenue Management player — see full profile in Startup Repository

companies.name: Refundle
companies.whatTheyDo: Automated refund processing for airlines and OTAs — handles disruption, schedule change, and compensation refunds end-to-end. SaaS for airline back-office and customer service teams. Interesting as a servicing-layer specialist in the post-booking value chain Amadeus's PSS competes in.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Competes adjacent to Amadeus Altea/Nevio servicing modules in the refund and disruption-management workflow. Doesn't displace PSS but takes share of the specific automation layer airlines are increasingly buying separately.
companies.amadeusThreat: Travel Servicing player — see full profile in Startup Repository

companies.name: Volantio
companies.whatTheyDo: AI-driven schedule disruption recovery and passenger re-accommodation platform for airlines. Optimizes irregular operations (IRROPS) response, voluntary rebooking offers, and operational recovery economics. Interesting because IRROPS/recovery is an airline-IT layer where Amadeus's Altea Departure Control and Servicing play.
companies.impactOnAmadeus: Direct competitor in airline-IT IRROPS and passenger-services workflows. If Volantio wins disruption-management mandates, it weakens Amadeus's grip on the operational layer of Altea and reduces upsell room for adjacent passenger-services modules.
